missions.name: Helios Coronagraph Occulter Realignment
missions.description: The Helios-A coronagraph's occulter disc has drifted a tenth of a degree — enough to flood the primary with direct sunlight. EVA over and re-shim the disc mounts before the next flare cycle.

missions.name: Cloud-Deck Relay Dish Reseat
missions.description: Acid-pitted mounting bolts are letting the Venus telemetry relay drift out of alignment. EVA across and reseat the main dish before the dropsonde network loses uplink.

missions.name: Dropsonde Carrier Cartridge Swap
missions.description: The dropsonde carrier's pyrotechnic deployment cartridges failed cert after the last acid-storm pass. Cycle out to the bus and swap the cartridge rack — surface science goes dark without it.

missions.name: L1 Uplink Backbone Reterm
missions.description: The main trunk on the Earth-Sun L1 relay is reading intermittent. Bring the crimp tool, EVA out, reterminate the backbone. Keep the old cable — the agency pays extra for failure analysis.

missions.name: Cubesat Cluster Firmware Patch
missions.description: Three-bird cubesat cluster in sun-synch missed the last OTA window. Dock with the master node, push the patch by hand, verify the other two sync up before you head home.

missions.name: L2 Observatory Mirror Phasing
missions.description: The L2 observatory's primary mirror segments drifted out of phase after a micrometeorite strike. EVA out, read the wavefront sensor, walk each segment in by hand.

missions.name: Sample-Return Beacon IMU Swap
missions.description: The beacon that will guide the next sample-return capsule home has a flaky inertial unit. EVA across, swap the IMU board, verify three-axis lock against the onboard reference.

missions.name: Methane Prospector Recalibration
missions.description: The methane-sniffing prospector satellite is reading dead-negatives across every orbit — almost certainly sensor drift. EVA and recalibrate the mass spectrometer against the onboard reference gas.

missions.name: Phobos Astrometric Array Focusing
missions.description: The Phobos-mounted astrometric array lost focus after the last moonquake. EVA over, walk the focus motor through its range, record the new optimum before the next transit window.

missions.name: Galilean Relay Firmware Flash
missions.description: The relay that keeps Io, Europa, and Ganymede on the same timebase is glitching under the radiation belt. Flash the rad-hardened firmware, pull the error logs, don't linger.

missions.name: Io Torus Plasma Probe Rebuild
missions.description: The sulfur-monitoring probe in the Io plasma torus is offline — mass analyzer ion-pitted past spec. Carry the replacement head out, swap it, realign the high-gain antenna toward Jupiter.

missions.name: Europa Plume Spectrograph Shutter Service
missions.description: The spectrograph watching Europa for water-plume eruptions has a stuck aperture shutter. EVA over and free it up — the astrobio team has been dark for four months.

missions.name: Titan Uplink Backbone Reterm
missions.description: The Titan relay uplink has been broadcasting garbage since last ring-plane crossing. Bring the diagnostic harness, EVA out, reterminate the main backbone cable.

missions.name: Ring-Particle Sampler Inlet Clearance
missions.description: The automated ring sampler is jammed — ice buildup on the collection aperture. EVA out with the scraper and clear the inlet by hand. Do not touch the filter.

missions.name: Enceladus Plume Spectrograph Cryo Purge
missions.description: The spectrograph watching Enceladus's south-polar plumes has a slow helium leak — detector's drifting warm and the organic bands are washing out. EVA over, purge the cryo line, re-pressurize. The astrobio team wants their amino-acid signal back.

missions.name: Tilt-Anomaly Probe Reaction Wheel Swap
missions.description: The axial-tilt monitoring probe took a gyro hit — one reaction wheel is frozen solid. EVA to the bus and swap the whole momentum stack. No rush. Nobody else is out here.

missions.name: Triton Telescope Actuator Release
missions.description: The Triton-orbiting exoplanet watcher's primary mirror actuator seized last month and nobody's been out to fix it. Deep-space money for deep-space work. The transit window is long — take your time.
